title: boretta cantabrica
intro: Boretta cantabrica, also known as Pyrenean spring sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to the Pyrenees region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and aiding in the removal of mucus from the lungs, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory conditions. Herbal preparations of Boretta cantabrica can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall respiratory health.
